Q: What region will Donald Trump say would have been taken over without US intervention, in remarks reported by 10 March 2026?
BG: Question Start Date: 2026-02-28. Donald Trump is the US president. Public foreign-policy statements often describe risks to specific parts of the world and the claimed effect of US action.
RC: Source of Truth : Resolve using an official transcript, video, or written statement of Donald Trump's relevant remarks if one is publicly released. If no official source is available, use contemporaneous reporting published no later than 10 March 2026 by a major newswire or similarly authoritative news outlet that directly quotes the statement. Resolution Date : This question resolves when the relevant remarks are publicly available or authoritatively reported, and no later than 10 March 2026. Accepted Answer Format : The answer must be the geographic region named in the statement, written as a standard place-name of one to three words.
  PRED: {'Middle East': 0.3, 'Caribbean': 0.12, 'Europe': 0.1, 'Latin America': 0.08, 'Gulf': 0.06}
=== 871156 ===
### 871156 | res=2026-03-05 | string (state name)
Q: What U.S. state outside Oklahoma will report four tornado deaths from the central U.S. storms by March 8, 2026?
BG: Question Start Date: 2026-02-27. In the coming days, severe storms may affect parts of the central United States, and early fatality reports may identify which states were hit hardest.
RC: Source of Truth : Official statements from state or local authorities in the affected areas, as carried by major wire services or state emergency channels by March 8, 2026. Resolution Date : This question resolves when the first widely cited state-by-state fatality breakdown for this tornado outbreak is publicly reported, no later than March 8, 2026. Accepted Answer Format : Provide the single U.S. state name reported as having four deaths outside Oklahoma. The answer must be the state name only.
  PRED: {'Texas': 0.16, 'Mississippi': 0.14, 'Arkansas': 0.12, 'Missouri': 0.12, 'Kansas': 0.1}

HEADLINE: Santa Ana winds expected to impact much of Southern California over next couple days
SOURCE: www.cbsnews.com
PUBLISHED: 2026-01-09 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2026-03-04 00:00:00
URL: https://www.cbsnews.com/losangeles/news/widespread-santa-ana-winds-gusts-advisory-southern-california/

Santa Ana winds expected to impact much of Southern California over next couple days

Southern California is expected to be impacted by strong, widespread Santa Ana winds over several days leading into the weekend. The National Weather Service says northerly winds will begin to affect parts of the region between 9 p.m. on Wednesday and 9 a.m. on Thursday. Areas along the coast could get gusts up to 35 mph and places like Northridge and Santa Clarita could get gusts up to 40 mph. Weather officials say some potential impacts include downed trees, power outages and dangerous driving conditions. A wind advisory will go into effect at 4 p.m. and remain until 9 a.m. on Thursday for areas like the Ojai Valley and Santa Barbara County. A high wind warning will go into effect at 2 a.m. and remain until 9 a.m. on Thursday for the Santa Monica Mountains and Malibu coast. CBS LA chief meteorologist Paul Deanno says stronger and more widespread winds will arrive on Friday. Ahead of the wind event, CBS LA has issued a Next Weather Alert for Friday through Sunday. Deanno says the next wave of wind will not be a typical Santa Ana profile but will get some of the same results. Some inland valleys will get wind gusts between 45 and 50 mph. Since Southern California received several inches of rain with winter storms earlier this year, fire danger is not a main concern.

HEADLINE: "Deadliest Catch" deckhand Todd Meadows dies after falling overboard in Alaska, Coast Guard says
SOURCE: www.cbsnews.com
PUBLISHED: 2026-03-04 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2026-03-04 00:00:00
URL: https://www.cbsnews.com/news/deadliest-catch-crewmember-dies-todd-meadows-falls-overboard-alaska/

"Deadliest Catch" deckhand Todd Meadows dies after falling overboard in Alaska, Coast Guard says

The fundraiser said Meadows, 25, died what doing what he loved best: crabbing on Alaska waters. "He was a determined dad to three amazing little boys who were his entire world," the GoFundMe site said. "Everything he did was for them — their smiles, their futures, and the memories they will carry with them."
The death is the latest for cast members of the show detailing dangerous crab fishing on the Bering Sea. The show first aired in 2005. In 2021, crewman Todd Kochutin, 30, died as a result of injuries he received while aboard the fishing vessel Patricia Lee, according to his obituary. Several other cast members have died of substance abuse or natural causes. They include Capt. Phil Harris of the fishing vessel Cornelia Marie, who died in 2010 at age 53 following a massive stroke, NPR reported.

Winter has a way of settling in—on your shelves, in your closet, and under the couch. While it may not feel like it yet, spring—and spring cleaning—is closer than you think. With a Dyson V8 Extra in your corner, that reset will be easier (and with the sale, cheaper) than ever. Weighing in at just under six pounds, this cordless vacuum feels less like a chore to bring out and more like something you’ll actually want to use. Even the Motorbar head is designed with de-tangling vanes, so you’re not stopping mid-cleaning sessions to untwist wrapped hair. Meadows, the ship’s deckhand, died on Feb. 25, at age 25. The U.S Coast Guard said on Tuesday Meadows died after he was reported to have fallen overboard around 170 miles north of Dutch Harbor, Alaska. He was recovered unresponsive by the crew around 10 minutes after going overboard. “Efforts to resuscitate Meadows were unsuccessful, and the crew brought his body to Dutch Harbor,” Chief Petty Officer Travis Magee, a spokesperson with the Coast Guard’s Arctic District, said.
